university.name: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University

gemini_summary: Virginia Tech's student housing market is experiencing significant demand growth, with consistent enrollment increases outpacing the pace of new supply. The most compelling signal is the robust +8.0% average annual rent growth over the last three years, indicating a persistent housing shortage. Developers should target new purpose-built student housing projects to capitalize on the high unmet demand and strong rental rate appreciation.

The University of Virginia market is experiencing substantial housing pressure, with student demand consistently outpacing the delivery of new residential units. The most compelling signal for this imbalance is the remarkable 3-year average annual rent growth of +9.6%, indicating severe undersupply. Developers should target new purpose-built student housing projects to capitalize on the market's high rent pressure and significant permit gap, particularly given the strong underlying demand.

The University of Tennessee-Knoxville market exhibits significant housing pressure, driven by strong student demand that consistently outpaces new purpose-built supply. The most critical signal for this market is the robust +10.7% average annual rent growth over the past three years, indicating severe undersupply and high demand elasticity. Developers should capitalize on this persistent supply-demand imbalance by pursuing new purpose-built student housing projects to meet the demonstrated need for modern accommodations.

university.name: North Carolina State University at Raleigh

The most compelling data signal is the impressive +9.4% annual rent growth over the past three years, indicating persistent undersupply relative to student demand. Developers have a clear opportunity to capitalize on this unmet demand by delivering purpose-built student housing that can command premium rents in a rapidly expanding university environment.

Despite stagnant enrollment growth, UNLV's market shows significant rent pressure and a high permit gap, indicating an acute undersupply of purpose-built student housing amidst a broader, active residential development landscape.
